rationale: The query/hyp engine is a moment-driven self-consistent threshold formula (Tc / cascade threshold fixed by ratio of low moments of an arbitrary, possibly heavy-tailed P(k), with anomalous behavior once a moment diverges), while the candidate applies the cavity method to map the solution-space landscape of graph coloring on fixed Erdos-Renyi/regular ensembles (clustering, condensation, freezing, colorability threshold, algorithmic hardness) — a different specific machinery with no treatment of arbitrary/heavy-tailed degree distributions or moment-divergence anomalies, so method_coherence is FALSE. The candidate's object (proper colorings of random graphs, a combinatorial constraint-satisfaction problem) is not the query's own object (Ising ferromagnet) but it is squarely within the same statistical-physics-of-random-graphs field as the query and bears no resemblance to hyp_generic_object (power-grid substations/cascading blackouts), so no genuine cross-field transfer has occurred and object_difference is FALSE.
